forecasts.reasoning: The question asks if there will be ANY SINGLE DAY out of a 42-day window (May 20 to June 30) where neither the New York Times nor the Wall Street Journal contains the word 'tariff' or 'tariffs' on their digital front pages. For this to resolve NO, at least one of these two publications must explicitly print the word 'tariff' on its front page every continuous day for 42 days straight. Given the typical ebb and flow of news cycles, the likelihood of this specific word making the front page of at least one of these papers every single day is vanishingly small. There will inevitably be days—especially on weekends or during other major news cycles like foreign policy crises, domestic politics, or general market updates—where the specific word 'tariff' does not make the top headlines or the 'What's News' column. Furthermore, the math heavily favors a YES: even if there is a 90% chance each day that at least one paper features the word, the probability of it occurring 42 days in a row is mathematically low (~1.1%). In reality, the daily probability is significantly lower. Thus, I am extremely confident it will resolve YES.
